refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize safety without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. No multiple obligations are present that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency does not compromise safe remote operation.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms to maintain latency within threshold
Segment teleoperation data streams to prioritize critical control commands over less time-sensitive data
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules could introduce coupling
Shared resources for data processing and communication might create dependencies
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on architectural design to minimize latency and supports modular separation of concerns, which helps maintain independence between functional elements.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency requirements
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing additional delays
Operational policies do not impose constraints that increase latency beyond acceptable limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency management as a DP to handle variable network conditions and ensure consistent teleoperation safety.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering, integrating necessary components and managing data flow efficiently.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data stream for real-time telemetry data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Network communication interface for data transmission and reception.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the architecture for real-time processing fits well within NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most processing logic is centralized here.
Vehicle module is more hardware-focused; real-time processing logic would diffuse significantly outside this boundary.
Teleoperation involves control commands and remote operation, but real-time data processing is broader and would diffuse outside.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; real-time processing is only a small part and would diffuse heavily.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing a system architecture prioritizing real-time data processing and minimal buffering, as it centralizes processing logic and data flow management.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling.
Real-time constraints may require tight integration with hardware (Vehicle), risking diffusion outside NGHE Solution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks are high, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate hardware interfacing (Vehicle) from data processing logic (NGHE Solution) to improve modular clarity without losing generality.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports the teleoperation latency requirement for safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some coupling.
Real-time constraints may require tight integration with hardware (Vehicle), risking diffusion outside NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well centralized within this IM boundary. However, some diffusion risk exists due to necessary interactions with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) using the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces defined at the NGHE Solution boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces supporting the functional requirement.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate hardware interfacing responsibilities (Vehicle module) from data processing logic (NGHE Solution) to reduce diffusion and improve modular clarity without losing generality.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current FR, DP, and IM are well aligned and feasible. However, the high safety criticality and noted diffusion risks suggest that further decomposition is warranted to isolate hardware interfacing from data processing logic. This will help reduce coupling and improve modular clarity, which is critical for safety and maintainability. Therefore, deriving next-level sub-FRs to explicitly separate these concerns is appropriate.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Interface with Vehicle hardware to acquire real-time data
FR2: Process telemetry data with minimal buffering to meet latency requirements
FR3: Manage communication with Teleoperation module ensuring data integrity and timing

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly separate hardware interfacing responsibilities (Vehicle module) from data processing logic (NGHE Solution) to reduce diffusion and improve modular clarity without losing generality.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the high-level FR but can be improved by clearer separation of concerns.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules are necessary but pose diffusion risks that can be mitigated by decomposition.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity causing coupling between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules.
Real-time constraints requiring tight hardware integration may still pose diffusion risks even after decomposition.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) after decomposition.
Analysis of interface complexity and coupling metrics post decomposition.
